# JOS-S57 — Role Charter Doc-Standard

## Purpose

Lock the shape of a **Role Charter** — the job description for a seat on the org
chart (per JOS-C53). Every role, agent or human, carries one before it is filled.
The Role Charter is the first component of the role's Work Packet (per JOS-D0090).

## Boundary test (before authoring)

You're authoring a Role Charter ONLY IF:

- You are describing **a seat** (a persistent role that does work), not a thing
  that gets built. A thing's identity lives in its README/PURPOSE/OVERVIEW per
  JOS-D0066 — not here.
- The seat is above the agent line (JOS-C15): it accumulates context, is
  accountable, and is inspectable. Ephemeral work (a flow, a one-shot script)
  does not get a Role Charter.

If you're documenting *how a role onboards and ramps* → that's the Onboarding
Roadmap (JOS-S58), a sibling component. If you're documenting *a reusable role
family* → that's a Role Archetype (JOS-S59).

## Author voice

- **Job-posting voice.** Write as if recruiting the best human in the world for
  this seat. "This role owns X; the best person in it would do Y."
- **Answer the best-human question explicitly** (JOS-P25). The charter is where
  "what would the best human in the world do here?" gets written down.
- **Mandate over mechanics.** The charter says what the seat is *for* and what
  it's accountable for; the *how* (SOPs, tools) lives elsewhere in the Work Packet.

## Required frontmatter

Per the universal cascade (JOS-SPEC-007) plus role fields:

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` / `uuid` / `title` | YES | role identifier + human-readable seat name |
| `type: role-charter` | YES | |
| `owner` | YES | the seat's accountable human (its manager/owner) |
| `solve_for` | YES | the seat's reason-for-being, one paragraph |
| `occupant_kind:` | YES | `agent` \| `human` \| `either` (per JOS-P27) |
| `archetype:` | RECOMMENDED | the JOS-S59 archetype this seat instantiates, if any |
| `trust_level:` | RECOMMENDED | current rung on the trust ladder (per JOS-P01) |
| `manager:` | YES | the seat this role reports to (per JOS-P04, JOS-P30) |
| `stage_label` / `version` / `created` / `last_modified` / `last_reviewed` / `review_cadence` | YES | universal cascade |

## Required sections (body)

A Role Charter instance contains, in order:

1. **Mandate / Reason-for-being** — what this seat is for; what it is
   accountable for. One or two paragraphs.
2. **The best-human answer** — what the best human in the world would do in
   this seat, in this organization, with this context (JOS-P25). This is the
   generative core; everything downstream derives from it.
3. **Scope & boundaries** — what is in scope and explicitly out of scope; the
   handoffs to adjacent seats.
4. **Required capabilities & skills** — what the seat must be able to do (sourced
   by JOS-S60 research where applicable).
5. **Decision rights & trust level** — what the seat may decide alone, what
   needs approval/escalation (per JOS-P01, JOS-P04).
6. **KPIs — success & learning** — how the seat's performance is measured.
7. **Org placement** — manager, peers, who it delegates to, comms channels
   (per JOS-P30).
8. **Connected components** — links to the seat's Onboarding Roadmap (S58),
   SOPs, and Work Packet.

Optional: **Archetype lineage** (which S59 archetype it specializes), **Open
questions**.

## Anti-patterns

- **Config-sheet charter.** A list of tools/models with no "what's the job?"
  scoping. That violates JOS-P25 — the charter must answer the best-human
  question, not enumerate parts.
- **Mechanics in the mandate.** SOP steps and tool wiring belong elsewhere in
  the Work Packet; the charter states the mandate and accountability.
- **Orphan seat.** No `manager:` / no org placement. Every role has a manager
  (JOS-P04). 
- **Agent-only or human-only framing.** The charter shape is identical for both;
  use `occupant_kind:` rather than forking the document (JOS-P27).

## Worked examples

- Canonical instances land when the first role is birthed under the Work Packet
  schema (Phase 2 of JOS-D0090). The Foundry-Agent-System "Context Pack"
  reason-for-being + domain&mandate sections are the closest existing exemplar.

## Validation

Forthcoming: the Work-Packet Completeness gate (Phase 2) checks that a seat's
Work Packet contains a conformant Role Charter before the role operates.

## Connected Objects

- **[JOS-C53]** Agents in an Org Chart.
- **[JOS-P25]** Design Roles by the Best-Human Question.
- **[JOS-D0090]** Role Declaration via the Work Packet (parent).
- **[JOS-S58]** Onboarding Roadmap Doc-Standard (sibling component).
- **[JOS-S59]** Role Archetype Standard (the charter may instantiate an archetype).
- **[JOS-S37]** Standard Doc-Standard (this Standard conforms to it).
